sections_cited: Section 25 of the Sindh Arms Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P No. S-44 of 2026 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objection-A. 2. For orders on maintainability of main case. 09-03-2026 Mr. Saeed Ahmed Panhwar, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Munawar Ali Abbasi, Astt. A.G..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Learned Assistant Advocate General files statements on behalf of respondents No.2, 4, 5 and 7, which are taken on record. In the said statements, it is submitted that the brothers of the petitioner are involved in criminal cases bearing FIR No.371/2025 under Sections 302, 109, 148, 149 P.P.C. and FIR No.386/2025 under Section 25 of the Sindh Arms Act, both registered at Police Station Mehar. It is further stated that due to the said criminal cases, the present petition has been filed with the object of extending protection to them. Learned AAG submits that the official respondents are bound to act strictly in accordance with law and that the Investigation Officer of the aforesaid cases is conducting investigation in accordance with law. He further submits that the official respondents have neither harassed the petitioner nor do they intend to do so in future. Conversely, learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner is a married woman and has no connection with the alleged activities of her brothers. After going through the statements filed on behalf of the official respondents and the submissions made by learned AAG, he seeks disposal of the petition. In view of the above, this petition is disposed of with the direction to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and not to cause any unnecessary harassment to the petitioner.

After going through the statements filed on behalf of the official respondents and the submissions made by learned AAG, he seeks disposal of the petition. In view of the above, this petition is disposed of with the direction to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and not to cause any unnecessary harassment to the petitioner. However, the official respondents shall remain at liberty to proceed in accordance with law with respect to FIR No.371/2025 under Sections 302, 109, 148, 149 P.P.C. and FIR No.386/2025 under Section 25 of the Sindh Arms Act, both registered at Police Station Mehar. It is clarified that nothing contained in this order shall prejudice the investigation or proceedings in the aforesaid criminal cases, which shall be conducted and decided strictly in accordance with law. Judge S.Ashfaq
